title: The Phoenix Project
lead: An interactive DevOps business simulation based on the bestselling novel — experience the challenges of IT operations and DevOps transformation first-hand.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: The Phoenix Project is a serious business simulation based on the book of the same name by Gene Kim, Kevin Behr, and George Spafford. Participants role-play within 'Parts Unlimited', a struggling retail company facing the classic tension between Development and Operations. Over three rounds, teams manage IT projects, handle incidents, and learn to apply DevOps principles under realistic business pressure. The simulation demonstrates that DevOps is not just a technology shift — it requires fundamental changes in how people behave, collaborate, and deliver value.

sections.heading: ITOM Perspective
sections.body: The Phoenix Project simulation is a powerful tool in ITOM engagements because it makes the 'why' behind operating model change tangible. Participants experience first-hand what happens when development and operations are siloed, when governance is too heavy, and when flow is blocked. This shared experience creates alignment and urgency for the operating model transformation — making subsequent ITOM design decisions easier to land because stakeholders have lived the pain and seen the alternative.

items.desc: participants assume roles across business (Retail Operations, HR, Finance) and IT (VP of IT Operations, IT Team members) within Parts Unlimited
each round represents a phase in the DevOps journey — from chaos and firefighting to structured flow and continuous improvement
teams face competing demands: backlogs of issues and features, business requests, SLA targets, and external disruptions
a critical business project that must be delivered on time while managing all operational demands — just like in real life
structured reflection after each round connects the simulation experience to real-world DevOps and operating model principles
Apply DevOps principles (flow, feedback, continuous learning) in realistic scenarios
Balance SLA requirements with project delivery deadlines
Demonstrate the business value of DevOps to non-technical stakeholders
Improve IT department efficiency by optimising team flow and reducing waste
Develop the behavioural and cultural skills needed for DevOps environments
Align business and IT responsibilities around shared outcomes
no prior DevOps knowledge required — suitable for both IT and business roles
available in-person and as online virtual simulation
Atos is certified trainer for The Phoenix Project simulation

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For more information about this training, contact arjen.bakker@atos.net
